title: myrtus marginata
intro: Myrtus marginata, also known as the Broad-leaved Myrtle or Narrow-leaved Myrtle, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, Myrtaceae. Native to the Mediterranean region, this plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The leaves and bark of the Myrtus marginata plant have been used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, coughs, and skin conditions, thanks to its antiseptic and astringent properties, which contribute to its numerous health benefits, including wound healing and immune system support. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, often combined with other herbs to create remedies for respiratory and digestive issues.
